name: Tasmanian Devil
summary: The Tasmanian devil is the world's largest carnivorous marsupial, found only on the island of Tasmania, Australia. Famous for its bone-crushing bite, eerie...

searchIntents: how big is a tasmanian devil

lifespan.captivity: Up to 8 years in human care

speed: Up to 15 mph for short distances

reproduction.offspringCount: Up to 30 joeys born; usually 2 to 4 survive in the pouch

funFacts: A Tasmanian devil's bite is so powerful relative to its body size that it can crush through bone with ease.
Newborn Tasmanian devil joeys are no bigger than a grain of rice and must crawl into the mother's pouch immediately after birth.
Tasmanian devils communicate through screams, growls, and yawns that can sound ferocious to predators.
They can eat up to 40 percent of their own body weight in a single feeding session.
Tasmanian devils are the only place on Earth where this species lives in the wild—the island of Tasmania.
A transmissible facial tumor disease called DFTD has killed more than 80 percent of the wild population since the 1990s.

faq.question: What is a Tasmanian devil?
faq.answer: A Tasmanian devil is the world's largest carnivorous marsupial. It is a stocky, black-furred animal found only on the island of Tasmania, Australia.

faq.question: Why is the Tasmanian devil called a devil?
faq.answer: Early European settlers named it a devil because of its loud, eerie screams and growls heard in the night, along with its aggressive feeding behavior.

faq.question: What do Tasmanian devils eat?
faq.answer: Tasmanian devils are carnivores that eat wombats, wallabies, carrion, birds, and reptiles. They often scavenge dead animals and eat bones, fur, and all.

faq.question: Where do Tasmanian devils live?
faq.answer: Tasmanian devils live only on the island of Tasmania, off the south coast of Australia. They were once found on mainland Australia but disappeared there thousands of years ago.

faq.question: How strong is a Tasmanian devil's bite?
faq.answer: The Tasmanian devil has the strongest bite relative to body size of any living mammal. It can crush bone and devour an entire carcass, leaving almost nothing behind.

faq.question: What is a baby Tasmanian devil called?
faq.answer: A baby Tasmanian devil is called a joey. Newborns are tiny—about the size of a grain of rice—and crawl into the mother's pouch to continue developing.

faq.question: How long do Tasmanian devils live?
faq.answer: Wild Tasmanian devils usually live 5 to 6 years. In captivity with regular food and veterinary care, they can live up to 8 years.

faq.question: Are Tasmanian devils endangered?
faq.answer: Yes. Tasmanian devils are listed as Endangered, mainly because of a contagious facial tumor disease called Devil Facial Tumor Disease (DFTD) that has devastated the wild population.

faq.question: Do Tasmanian devils screech?
faq.answer: Yes. Tasmanian devils make loud screeches, growls, and coughs, especially when competing for food. The sounds can seem terrifying at night in the bush.

faq.question: Are Tasmanian devils nocturnal?
faq.answer: Yes. Tasmanian devils are mainly active at night when they forage and scavenge for food. During the day they rest in dense bush, logs, or burrows.

faq.question: What is Devil Facial Tumor Disease?
faq.answer: Devil Facial Tumor Disease is a contagious cancer spread through biting. It causes tumors on the face that prevent the animal from eating, eventually leading to death.

faq.question: What threatens Tasmanian devils?
faq.answer: The biggest threat is Devil Facial Tumor Disease. Other threats include vehicle strikes on roads, habitat loss, and introduced predators such as foxes.
